name: Chocolate Chip and Currant Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: 7 ounces Semi-sweet chocolate, cut into 1/4 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Heat currants in Cognac in a small saucepan until just warm. Cover mixture and let stand for one hour. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter an 8-inch fluted tube cake pan. Dust with flour. Sift together 2 cups of flour, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon into a medium bowl. Measure out the half and half into a small dish and drain the soaking liquid from the currants into the half and half. Add the vanilla extract.
In another small bowl combine the chocolate pieces, the currants, and the 2 3/4 cups flour. In a large bowl, with an electric mixer, cream the butter. Add the sugar and beat until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time. Then mix the flour and half and half mixture alternately into the butter. Begin and end with the flour. Using a spatula, fold in the chocolate mixture.
Spoon batter into prepared pan and bake in the center of the oven until springy to the touch, about 1 hour. Cool cake in pan on a rack for 15 minutes. Gently run a knife around the edge of the cake and invert onto the rack. Cool completely. Sprinkle with powdered sugar or drizzle glaze over the top allowing it to run down the sides. Glaze: To make glaze, blend sugar, butter, cream, and Cognac in a processor until fluffy.
